trivia_bullets.text: Retreat to Valakut is the red member of the five-card BFZ Retreat enchantment cycle, each depicting a named Zendikar location and having a landfall trigger. The full cycle: Retreat to Emeria (white, 44), Retreat to Coralhelm (blue, 82), Retreat to Hagra (black, 121), Retreat to Valakut (red, 153), Retreat to Kazandu (green, 186) [Scryfall: BFZ set search].

Valakut, the Molten Pinnacle is described as the largest and most active volcanic peak of Zendikar's Crown range. It is the plane's most famous landmark with a longstanding card of the same name from the original Zendikar set (2009), which generates 3 damage per Mountain entering when controlling five or more Mountains — a card that became a Modern combo staple [Scryfall: Valakut, the Molten Pinnacle, Zendikar 228; WebSearch snippet].

The card's two landfall modes — +2/+0 or 'can't block this turn' — make it a combat-efficiency tool rather than a card-advantage engine. EDHREC rank 15614 suggests moderate Commander inclusion; it sees more use in landfall-focused decks than standalone builds [Scryfall: edhrec_rank].

Retreat to Valakut has no flavor text [Scryfall: no flavor_text field], unlike some of its cycle-mates. The art (painted by Kieran Yanner) does the location-establishing work that flavor text would otherwise carry. Art depicts the volcanic canyon landscape of Valakut purely as environment, no characters present.

Legal in Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, and Commander. The card sees fringe use in Scapeshift-adjacent Pioneer lists where landfall triggers help push through blockers on the combo turn, but Retreat to Valakut is not a centerpiece of any tier-1 deck [Scryfall; WebSearch: Valakut combo Pioneer/Modern].
